title: Perymenium jelskii
intro: Perymenium jelskii is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which are beneficial in treating various health issues such as pain, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Perymenium jelskii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Perymenium jelskii is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Perymenium jelskii is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family, such as its flower structure and seed production. Perymenium jelskii is further classified within the subfamily Asteroideae, which includes many other species of flowering plants. The genus Perymenium is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and Perymenium jelskii is one of the many species within this genus.
